title: Visiting Research Fellowship for a prospective PhD student in Migration Studies with a focus on informal work
summary: Short-term visiting research fellowship at the University for Continuing Education Krems for a prospective PhD student developing a proposal on informal work and migration in Europe. The fellowship supports preparation of a DOC funding application, with guidance from migration scholars and possible support for physical presence in Krems.

title: Early-Stage Researcher / PhD student in Migration Studies — ERC project on Brazil and Spain regularisation policies
provider: IMISCOE Jobs & Opportunities / University for Continuing Education Krems
summary: Fully funded PhD / early-stage researcher position at the University for Continuing Education Krems within an ERC project on ethical dilemmas of migrant solidarity. The researcher will work on national migration policies in Brazil and Spain, using qualitative research and contributing to a cumulative PhD dissertation.

title: Postdoctoral Researcher in Migration Studies — ERC Consolidator Grant, US and Latin America
provider: IMISCOE Jobs & Opportunities / University for Continuing Education Krems
summary: Three-year postdoctoral researcher position at the University for Continuing Education Krems within an ERC project on ethical dilemmas of migrant solidarity. The postdoc will lead a work package on sanctuary/solidarity cities in the United States and Latin America, using qualitative fieldwork and contributing to theory-building on migration governance and solidarity.

title: 26th Sanremo Summer School
summary: Two-week in-person summer school in Sanremo organised by the International Institute of Humanitarian Law. It is relevant for people working on protection, humanitarian law, human rights and related fields. The course is in English and provides an official certificate.

title: Summer School on Migration and Human Rights
summary: One-week in-person summer school in Rome on migration and human rights, organised by UNICRI and John Cabot University. It covers legal, policy, social and technological dimensions of migration governance, including asylum law, migrant protection, trafficking, AI and digital border management, algorithmic decision-making, disinformation and public narratives on migration.

title: HORIZON-CL2-2026-01-TRANSFO-08 — Strengthened implementation of the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum and a focus on inclusion, integration, and health
summary: Horizon Europe research funding topic on the implementation of the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum, with a focus on evidence-based migration governance, asylum management, age assessment of minors, resettlement, legal pathways, migrant and refugee health data, and long-term social inclusion and integration strategies.

title: Luxembourg subsidies for intercultural projects supporting third-country national integration
provider: European Commission / Luxembourg Ministry of Family, Solidarity, Living Together and Hospitality
summary: Luxembourg funding scheme for non-profit organisations, foundations and social impact entities developing projects that support intercultural living together, anti-racism, equal opportunities and the integration of third-country nationals. Applications can be submitted during the year, with a key deadline on 8 October 2026.

title: Socio-labour inclusion and empowerment of migrant women: 2026 public calls
provider: Ministero del Lavoro e delle Politiche Sociali
summary: Italian public funding calls for projects supporting the socio-labour inclusion and empowerment of migrant women. The calls cover women victims of trafficking, serious labour exploitation and gender-based violence, qualification of domestic and care work, and inclusion of migrant women who are unemployed, inactive or in precarious employment.

title: Call for input: Children's right to family life in the context of migration and asylum policies
provider: OHCHR / Special Rapporteur on the human rights of migrants
summary: OHCHR call for inputs by the Special Rapporteur on the human rights of migrants on children's right to family life in the context of migration and asylum policies. It is relevant for organisations, researchers and practitioners with evidence on family separation, child protection, asylum procedures, migration enforcement and children's rights.

title: Assistant Professor — Centre of Migration Research, University of Warsaw
provider: Centre of Migration Research, University of Warsaw
summary: Assistant Professor position at the Centre of Migration Research, University of Warsaw. This is a faculty-rank academic job in a dedicated migration research centre, making it highly relevant for researchers seeking positions beyond the postdoc/fellowship stage.

title: Call for Proposals - Migration Partnerships
geography: EU; Southern Neighbourhood; Sub-Saharan Africa; Silk Route; Central Asia; Egypt; Morocco; Tunisia; Pakistan; Bangladesh
summary: Rolling call under the Migration Partnership Facility supporting EU migration priorities with partner countries and projects aligned with EU Talent Partnerships. It can fund migration governance, legal/labour mobility, migrant protection, asylum policy, reintegration, diaspora-related activities and capacity-building. It is highly relevant but administratively complex and not designed for small informal organisations.

title: Community Connection Grant
summary: Small local community grant from Hume City Council supporting activities that improve access, inclusion, equity, participation, community connection, health and wellbeing. It is not migration-specific, but it is relevant for migrant-led, multicultural or refugee-supporting community groups in Hume if their project benefits local residents and fits the inclusion/community connection objectives.

title: Migration Policy Institute Fall 2026 Research Internship
geography: United States; remote possible; global applicants possible
summary: Fall 2026 paid research internship at the Migration Policy Institute. Research interns can work across MPI programme areas including U.S. immigration, international migration, immigrant integration, Latin America and Caribbean migration, and refugee/forced displacement. This is a strong entry-level migration-sector opportunity for students, graduates and early-career professionals interested in policy research.

title: IMISCOE Spring Conference 2027 — Call for Papers
provider: IMISCOE / Global Migration Institute, Toronto Metropolitan University
summary: Call for Papers for the 2027 IMISCOE Spring Conference in Toronto, hosted by the Global Migration Institute at Toronto Metropolitan University. The conference focuses on digital transformations in migration governance and migration research, including AI, biometric systems, digital borders, migrant rights, research ethics and participatory/community-based approaches.

title: RUNOMI PhD Autumn School 2026
summary: Three-day autumn school for PhD candidates working on migration and health. It addresses methodological, ethical and empirical dilemmas in research and healthcare, and is hosted by RUNOMI at Radboud University and Radboudumc in Nijmegen.

title: Making Europe a global magnet for talent - Attracting and retaining students, researchers and high-skilled workers from outside the EU
summary: Horizon Europe research funding topic on attracting and retaining students, researchers and high-skilled workers from outside the EU. The topic is strongly migration-relevant but primarily suited to research consortia rather than small organisations applying alone.

title: Proteus Fund / RISE Together — 2026 Small Grants Fund
summary: Proteus Fund / RISE Together Fund is offering a 2026 Small Grants Fund. The opportunity is relevant to civil society organisations and initiatives working on rights, inclusion, social justice, and affected communities, with a listed deadline of 15 June 2026.

title: IOM — Consultant, Legal Identity and Civil Registration Toolbox
summary: IOM is advertising a consultancy related to legal identity and civil registration tools. The opportunity is relevant to migration governance, legal identity, civil registration, protection, and technical support for policy and programming, with a listed deadline of 15 June 2026.

title: OHCHR — Reconstruction and Internally Displaced Persons
provider: OHCHR / Special Rapporteur on the human rights of internally displaced persons
summary: OHCHR is inviting inputs for a report on reconstruction and internally displaced persons in conflict and post-conflict contexts. The call is relevant to displacement, protection, human rights, and policy evidence, with a listed deadline of 15 June 2026.

title: FCDO — Uganda Refugee Support, Empowerment and Transformation ReSET Project
provider: Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office / British High Commission Kampala
summary: The UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office is advertising the Uganda Refugee Support, Empowerment and Transformation project. The opportunity is relevant to refugee support, protection, livelihoods, and humanitarian-development programming in Uganda, with a listed deadline of 16 June 2026.

title: ICMPD — Internship, Research Unit
provider: International Centre for Migration Policy Development
summary: ICMPD is advertising an internship in its Research Unit. The opportunity is relevant to early-career candidates interested in migration policy, research support, European migration governance, and applied migration analysis, with a listed deadline of 17 June 2026.

title: IOM — Case Manager, Assisted Voluntary Return
summary: IOM is advertising a case manager role connected to Assisted Voluntary Return. The opportunity is relevant to migration casework, return and reintegration support, migrant assistance, and operational migration programming, with a listed deadline of 18 June 2026.

title: ICMPD ENHANCER PRO — Start-Up Grant Scheme Türkiye
summary: ICMPD / ENHANCER PRO is offering a start-up grant scheme in Türkiye. The opportunity is relevant to entrepreneurship, livelihoods, economic inclusion, and support for communities affected by displacement and migration, with a listed deadline of 19 June 2026.

title: Oxford Refugee Studies Centre — International Online School in Forced Migration, June 2026
provider: Refugee Studies Centre, University of Oxford
summary: The Refugee Studies Centre at the University of Oxford is offering an International Online School in Forced Migration. The programme is relevant to students, practitioners, researchers, and policy actors working on refugees, displacement, protection, and forced migration, with a listed deadline of 22 June 2026.

title: UNHCR — Nansen Refugee Award 2026 Nominations
summary: UNHCR is inviting nominations for the 2026 Nansen Refugee Award. The opportunity is relevant to individuals, groups, and organisations supporting refugees, displaced people, and stateless people, with a listed deadline of 23 June 2026.

title: 2027 IMISCOE Spring Conference — Studying Migration in the Digital Era
provider: IMISCOE / Global Migration Institute at Toronto Metropolitan University
summary: IMISCOE and the Global Migration Institute at Toronto Metropolitan University are organising the 2027 IMISCOE Spring Conference on migration in the digital era. The opportunity is relevant to migration researchers working on digitalisation, mobility, governance, and migration studies, with a listed deadline of 26 June 2026.

title: Portugal 2030 / PESSOAS-2026-7 — Portuguese Language Learning for Foreign Citizens
summary: Portugal 2030 / PESSOAS 2030 is supporting Portuguese language learning for foreign citizens. The opportunity is relevant to integration, language access, inclusion, and support services for migrants and foreign residents, with a listed deadline of 26 June 2026.

title: FCDO — Jordan Humanitarian and Systems Strengthening Programme, Protection Component
provider: UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office
summary: The UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office is advertising a protection component within the Jordan Humanitarian and Systems Strengthening Programme. The opportunity is relevant to humanitarian protection, forced displacement, refugee response, and systems-strengthening work in Jordan, with a listed deadline of 28 June 2026.

title: UNHCR — Innovation Accelerator 2026
summary: The UNHCR Innovation Service is running the 2026 Innovation Accelerator. The opportunity is relevant to innovation, refugee protection, displacement response, community-led solutions, and practical support for forcibly displaced people, with a listed deadline of 12 July 2026.

title: ARUA — Early-Career Research Fellowships, Migration and Mobility
summary: The African Research Universities Alliance is offering early-career research fellowships on migration and mobility. The opportunity is relevant to researchers working on African migration, mobility, development, and regional research collaboration, with a listed deadline of 15 July 2026.

title: African Union ECOSOCC — 5th Permanent General Assembly for African and Diaspora CSOs
opportunity_type: civil society participation / diaspora governance opportunity
summary: African Union ECOSOCC is convening the 5th Permanent General Assembly for African and Diaspora civil society organisations. The opportunity is relevant to diaspora engagement, civil society participation, African governance, and transnational civic networks, with a listed deadline of 15 July 2026.

title: Consultant Type B - Migration Health Nurse
summary: IOM is advertising a Migration Health Nurse consultancy/job opportunity in Tunisia through its official Impactpool careers page. The role is relevant to migration health, service delivery, and operational support for migrant and mobile populations, with a listed deadline of 26 June 2026.

title: Italy: Call for projects on socio-labour inclusion and empowerment of migrant women
provider: Ministero del Lavoro e delle Politiche Sociali
summary: Italian public authorities are advertising a call for projects supporting socio-labour inclusion and empowerment of migrant women. The opportunity is relevant to migration, integration, civil society/service providers, and migrant women's inclusion, with a listed deadline of 2026-06-23.

summary: ReliefWeb lists this job opportunity as relevant to migration, displacement, refugee protection, or humanitarian response. The role is associated with Médecins du Monde, geography: Mexico, with a listed deadline of 2026-06-13.

summary: ReliefWeb lists this job opportunity as relevant to migration, displacement, refugee protection, or humanitarian response. The role is associated with International Rescue Committee, geography: Kenya, with a listed deadline of 2026-08-31.

title: MiReKoc / BU / CfD International Summer School 2026: Crossing Regions, Shaping Lives — African Migration Corridors in Global Perspective
summary: International summer school on African migration corridors, organised by MiReKoc, Boğaziçi University and CfD. The opportunity is migration-specific and has an application deadline of 26 June 2026.

title: 2026 IMISCOE Annual Conference PhD Activities
summary: PhD activities linked to the 2026 IMISCOE Annual Conference in Oslo. The opportunity is migration-specific through IMISCOE and has a deadline of 24 June 2026.

provider: Administration for Children and Families / Office of Refugee Resettlement
summary: Funding opportunity for Home Study and Post-Release Services for Unaccompanied Alien Children, listed by the Administration for Children and Families / Office of Refugee Resettlement on Simpler Grants.
